texto: When you reached the point of losing your reasoning, that's called falling in love. Actually, those who have lost their reasoning chase blindly without thinking about the risks. They run and run like confused wild pigs. That's why, most of the time, they end up getting hurt. -- Sagan
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Yuuri Eda: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “When you reached the point of losing your reasoning, that's called falling in love. Actually, tho…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, falling-in-love, love pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e love.

frase_original: When you reached the point of losing your reasoning, that's called falling in love. Actually, those who have lost their reasoning chase blindly without thinking about the risks. They run and run like confused wild pigs. That's why, most of the time, they end up getting hurt. -- Sagan

texto: Humans are really stupid. If we don't fall and get hurt, we won't learn our lesson. Just like a kid repeatedly falling.No matter how many times we fail, or how many times we've fallen. Humans will stand once again, smiling ruefully at our grazed knees before continuing onwards. This time we will not run from our pain.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Yuuri Eda: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Humans are really stupid. If we don't fall and get hurt, we won't learn our lesson. Just like a k…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, failing, failure pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de failure e human.

Just like a kid repeatedly falling.No matter how many times we fail, or how many times we've fallen. Humans will stand once again, smiling ruefully at our grazed knees before continuing onwards.
